The '''Battle of Kolatill''' was a small [[Space warfare/Legends|engagement]] fought in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] at the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kolatill]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The engagement was contested between the combined forces of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and the [[Kolatill Defense Force]], and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]] allied to [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], the Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] of the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Sarne was [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|ousted]] from his [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]], by [[New Republic task force (Kal'Shebbol)|New Republic forces]]. In response, Kolatill overthrew its [[Tetrum|Imperial governor]] and declared allegiance to the New Republic. This act of defiance resulted in the [[Ton-Falk-class escort carrier|''Ton-Falk''-class escort carrier]] ''[[Kathol Protector]]'' [[Bombing of Domaz|bombing]] the capital [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Domaz]].

Following the bombing, the [[Kolatill Council]] sent out a [[Distress signal/Legends|distress signal]] to the New Republic forces at Kal'Shebbol. The [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' responded to the call, and was in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of the planet when the ''Kathol Protector'' returned to assault Kolatill for a second [[Time/Legends|time]] using [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]] armed with [[pathogenic bomb]]s. The New Republic [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] and its [[FarStar starfighter squadron|starfighter complement]] engaged the Imperial force, aided by the local [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] of the Kolatill Defense Force. The battle was fought both in the planet's orbit and within its [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], with the ''FarStar'' and a number of its [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] engaging the ''Kathol Protector'' directly while the Kolatill Defense Force and the remainder of the corvette's screening force engaged the escort [[Carrier/Legends|carrier's]] [[Bomber/Legends|bomber]] force. The joint effort between the New Republic and the Kolatill Defense Force managed to neutralize the threat posed by the pathogenic weapons and drive off the escort carrier. However, the battle was not without cost to the victors—[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Keleman Ciro]], [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]] of the ''FarStar'', was captured by Imperial forces during the battle, and his younger brother, [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Noell Ciro]], was killed in the engagement.

==Prelude==
In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberated]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]]—[[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]—freeing it from the rule of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]]. Shortly after, the neighboring planet of [[Kolatill]] followed suit and ousted its own Imperial [[Governor/Legends|governor]], [[Tetrum]]. In retaliation for this act, the [[Ton-Falk-class escort carrier|''Ton-Falk''-class escort carrier]] ''[[Kathol Protector]]''—which was part of the Moff's [[Moff Sarne's forces|military forces]]—arrived in the [[Kolatill system/Legends|system]] and launched [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]]. Targeting the capital [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Domaz]], the [[Bomber/Legends|bombers]] unleashed a [[Bombing of Domaz|heavy assault]] that left the city in flames before withdrawing. The [[Kolatill Council|government]] of the planet issued an emergency [[Distress signal/Legends|distress call]] to New Republic forces stationed at Kal'Shebbol, pleading for assistance.<ref name="O" />

The New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'', charged with [[DarkStryder Campaign|tracking down]] Sarne after he had fled Kal'Shebbol with the majority of his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], intercepted the distress call while in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of the planet. Since the ''FarStar'' was the only [[Starship/Legends|ship]] capable of heading to Kolatill immediately, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Keleman Ciro]] gave orders to make for the planet. Governor [[Monjai]], the newly installed New Republic official in charge of Kal'Shebbol, promised to send relief ships as soon as possible.<ref name="O" />

After several days in [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], the ''FarStar'' arrived at Kolatill, where Captain Ciro immediately dispatched teams to the surface to assist in relief and recovery operations. He also ordered the ''FarStar'' to constantly scan the [[Star system/Legends|system]] and maintain a combat air patrol. The Kolatill Council, which was hoping to ally itself with the New Republic, agreed to meet with Ciro, and dispatched a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] to ferry him to the surface.<ref name="O" />

==The battle==
Captain Ciro boarded the diplomatic shuttle and was in transit to Kolatill's surface when the escort [[Carrier/Legends|carrier]] ''Kathol Protector'' emerged from hyperspace. The Imperial vessel had returned to the planet to conduct a second [[Space warfare/Legends|strike]] utilizing a simple battle plan. The TIE bombers would spread across the planet and attack as many of the cities and population areas as possible. Each bomber had been loaded with highly volatile [[pathogenic bomb]]s that would spread [[Poison/Legends|poison]] over a large area when detonated. The bomb's payload was so toxic that each of the prime components were stored separately and only mixed together immediately before deployment. Kept apart, the individual components were harmless. Arriving to find the ''FarStar'' in orbit, the escort carrier's attack plan was altered slightly to deal with the unexpected ship.<ref name="O" />

The Imperial vessel launched its [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] complement, which consisted of two [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] of TIE bombers and four squadrons of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]]. Both of the bomber squadrons angled toward the planet, while the four fighter squadrons [[Starfighter combat/Legends|moved to intercept]] the ''FarStar'' and the diplomatic shuttle. The TIE Fighters split their attack; one squadron engaged the diplomatic shuttle, using their superior numbers to herd the small vessel toward the ''Kathol Protector''. The other three squadrons were assigned to harass the New Republic [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] and prevent it from launching its own [[FarStar starfighter squadron|fighters]].<ref name="O" />

[[File:Noell_Ciro.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Noell Ciro, who sacrificed his own life to defend Kolatill]]
Pursued by TIE Fighters, Ciro's shuttle was slowly directed toward the escort carrier. Once in range, the vessel's [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] generators locked onto the shuttle and pulled it into the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bay, capturing the Captain. The ''FarStar'', outnumbered by TIE Fighters, fought a desperate defensive action under the command of the [[Executive officer/Legends|First Officer]], [[Kaiya Adrimetrum]].<ref name="O" />

With TIE bombers descending to the surface, the [[Kolatill Defense Force]] scrambled its own few TIE Fighters and sent them to intercept the incoming Imperial ships. Fortunately for the citizens of the planet, several of the ''FarStar''{{'s}} [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] were also on the surface. The [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]], including those flown by [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Ranna Gorjaye]] and [[Noell Ciro]], had been assigned to escort duty for the two [[Transport/Legends|transport]] shuttles that were being used to ferry relief supplies and personnel to the planet.<ref name="O" />

The combined force of TIE Fighters and X-wings engaged the bombers in Kolatill's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. As a New Republic fighter destroyed the first bomber, its [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] detected a small concentration of highly toxic poison in the immediate area of the explosion. Suddenly aware that the bombers were carrying pathogenic bombs that could kill millions if allowed to detonate, Kolatill's defenders increased their efforts. During the engagement, Noell Ciro's X-wing took heavy damage that rendered his weapons system inoperative. His sensors detected a bomber nearing its target site, and he realized that he was the only fighter in range that could stop it. Deprived of weapons, Ciro set a collision course and rammed the bomber. The resulting explosion destroyed the Imperial ship and its payload but also [[Death/Legends|cost Ciro his own life]]. The entire bomber force was eliminated without any of the craft releasing their payloads; with its bomber force defeated, the ''Kathol Protector''{{'s}} primary mission was a failure. Recalling its TIE Fighters, the escort carrier disengaged and made the jump to hyperspace.<ref name="O" />

==Aftermath==
With the Imperial attack repulsed, Kolatill was indebted to the New Republic. The members of the Kolatill Council provided the crew of the ''FarStar'' with the [[Astrogation/Legends|coordinates]] to the planet [[Gandle Ott]] to allow them to continue their hunt for Sarne,<ref name="O" /> as the Moff had wiped all navigational records of the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] from the [[Computer/Legends|computers]] in his [[Imperial Government Complex|headquarters]] on Kal'Shebbol.<ref name="TSB">{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> The ''FarStar''{{'s}} First Officer, Kaiya Adrimetrum, assumed the captaincy of the vessel following Captain Ciro's capture, with the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Security officer/Legends|Security Officer]] [[Gorak Khzam]] taking over her role as second-in-command.<ref name="O" /> Following his capture, Ciro was delivered to the [[Q'Maere Research Facility]] in the [[Kathol Rift/Legends|Kathol Rift]], where Sarne had established a [[Prison/Legends|prison]], for interrogation.<ref name="KR">''[[The Kathol Rift]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Battle of Kolatill is featured in the [[West End Games]] ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' adventure ''[[Omens]]'', which was the first scenario in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', published in [[1995]]. Although the battle can be run in any way the gamemaster and players see fit, the capture of Captain Keleman Ciro and the death of Noell Ciro are required events, as the rest of the [[The DarkStryder Campaign (series)|series]] continues with the assumption that Noell Ciro is dead, Keleman Ciro is captured, and that Kaiya Adrimetrum commands the ''FarStar''.<ref name="O" /> A later plot point in ''[[Harm's Way]]'', an adventure published in the supplement ''[[The Kathol Rift]]'', which continues the adventures of the ''FarStar'', deals with Keleman Ciro's rescue.<ref name="HW">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Harm's Way|book=The Kathol Rift}}</ref>
